summary:
The patient has a higher chance of experiencing complications related to Eisenmengers syndrome than any mentioned alternative conditions given their unique physiological challenges exacerbating under stress like pregnancy; thus it poses greater threat regarding maternal outcomes specifically highlighted through multiple studies reviewed here focusing primarily upon high-risk scenarios associated directly or indirectly via underlying pathophysiology characteristic only seen prominently within Eisenmenger's context among those options provided initially posed as possibilities. Agent1 concludes that Eisenmenger's Syndrome leads to the most elevated maternal mortality rates because of its specific hemodynamic instabilities making pregnancies extremely risky. \boxed{A}

The summary focuses solely on highlighting how Eisenmenger's Syndrome presents distinctively challenging physiology especially when subjected to stresses such as pregnancy resulting in heightened maternal morbidity/mortality concerns supported extensively across various medical literatures consulted herein detailing comparative analyses between different cardiac abnormalities including mitral valve issues co-arctations etc... Thus concluding option 'a' being correct choice reflecting greatest potential harm towards expectant mothers amongst presented alternatives. Key takeaways emphasize severity & unpredictability inherent nature of disease process involved coupled alongside lackluster prognosis even after modern advancements indicating persistent dangers warranting utmost caution from healthcare providers managing affected pregnant women . 

In conclusion, considering all factors discussed above about each respective type of CHD along side extensive review conducted , I firmly believe eisenmengers remains top contender posing maximum danger hence justifying selection made earlier while answering original query asked at start concerning identification of particular form chd linked closely toward worst possible outcome pertaining overall health status / survival prospects postpartum irrespective individual case variations observed clinically speaking .
